checks.name: Source-fix intent
checks.remediation: State whether `/app/environment` source code must be fixed, or explicitly say output-only work is allowed.

checks.name: Verifier command/pipeline visibility
checks.remediation: Name the command or pipeline the verifier reruns at the public contract level, without naming the patch site.

checks.name: Regenerated vs static output
checks.remediation: If tests regenerate outputs, say static/manual output writes are insufficient and the normal pipeline must regenerate the artifacts.

checks.name: Inputs/profiles/argument splits
checks.remediation: Move tested profiles, labels, modes, argument splits, and CLI flags into instruction.md or visible environment docs/source help.

checks.name: Schemas/types/cardinality
checks.remediation: Give output keys, types, object/array distinctions, byte lengths, and cardinalities a visible contract home.

checks.name: Formulas/constants
checks.remediation: Disclose externally tested formulas, numeric constants, tolerances, byte sizes, digest algorithms, and profile constants at the contract level.

checks.name: Direct library-call expectations
checks.remediation: If tests call library functions directly, state that library/module-level behavior must be correct, not only the CLI output.

checks.name: Subsystem-level entry point
checks.remediation: Give a fair subsystem, directory, or component scope; do not rely only on metaphors or exact patch paths.

checks.name: Metaphor-only requirements
checks.remediation: Replace coined metaphors with observable behavior, concrete artifacts, or standard domain vocabulary.

checks.name: Active misdirection
checks.remediation: If the instruction names a driver/entry point but tests bypass it, state naturally that driver-only changes are not enough.
